properties.verseOrder: V1 V2 C3 V4 C3 V5 C3 V6 C3 V7 C3 V8 C3 V9 C3 V10 C3

verse.name: V1
verse.lines: Here so many are taking vacation, To the mountains, the lakes or the sea; Where they rest from their cares and their worries, What a wonderful time that must be.

verse.name: V2
verse.lines: But it seems not my lot to be like them, I must toil thru the heat and the cold, Seeking out the lost sheep in the mountains, Bringing wanderers back to the fold.

verse.name: C3
V4
verse.lines: Sitting down by the banks of the river; 'Neath the shade of the ever green tree, I shall rest from my burdens forever, Won't you spend your vacation with me?

verse.name: V5
verse.lines: Now some day I shall take my vacation To the city John tells us about; With its foundation walls all so precious, Where from gladness of heart I shall shout!

verse.name: V6
verse.lines: O, no sights ever witnessed by mortals Can compare with the glories up there. I shall spend my vacation with Jesus, On the place He went on to prepare.

verse.name: V7
V8
verse.lines: Soon there's going to be an excursion, I am booked for a ride in the air, You're invited to share my vacation, And the feast with our Bridegroom to share.

verse.name: V9
V10
verse.lines: I shall dwell with my Savior forever With Him sit on His Heavenly throne; All the days will be one, long vacation, When my Savior takes me to His home.
